title: Anthropic vs OpenAI: 271 bugs found, three of them real
desc: Daily AI news satire. Rival labs launch competing cybersecurity AIs — Claude Mythos flags 271 Firefox bugs (3 real), OpenAI answers with Daybreak.

beats.name: dojo-cold-open-two-warriors
beats.narration: The two largest A.I. labs both launched competing cybersecurity divisions this month.

beats.name: mythos-slashes-firefox-scroll
beats.narration: Anthropic's new Claude Mythos model was turned loose on the Firefox codebase.

beats.name: 271-shards-rise
beats.narration: Per Mozilla, it identified two hundred seventy-one vulnerabilities.

beats.name: mozilla-fox-spirit-quote
beats.narration: Mozilla called the model just as capable as an elite human researcher.

beats.name: three-shards-glow-gold
beats.narration: Mozilla's official advisory credits three of those as actual security CVEs.

beats.name: smith-receives-curl-scroll
beats.narration: A separate test was run on the curl codebase. The maintainer reviewed five reported issues.

beats.name: smith-returns-one-low
beats.narration: Three were already known. One wasn't a security bug at all. One was real, rated low.

beats.name: smith-quote-card
beats.narration: The curl maintainer Daniel Stenberg said the hype around the model was, in his words, primarily marketing.

beats.name: altman-enters-three-blades
beats.narration: OpenAI's response, announced two days ago, is called Daybreak.

beats.name: altman-quote-chyron
beats.narration: Sam Altman said A.I. is already good, and about to get super good, at cybersecurity.

beats.name: partner-army-marches
beats.narration: Eight enterprise security firms have signed on as launch partners.

beats.name: both-warriors-return-to-forge
beats.narration: Both labs will continue shipping the larger coding models that write next quarter's bugs.

sources.attribution: Mozilla says it patched 271 Firefox vulnerabilities thanks to Anthropic's Claude Mythos (Engadget)
Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means (SecurityWeek)
Daybreak is OpenAI's answer to the AI arms race in cybersecurity (CyberScoop)
OpenAI Launches Daybreak for AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation (The Hacker News)

transcript: The two largest A.I. labs both launched competing cybersecurity divisions this month. Anthropic's new Claude Mythos model was turned loose on the Firefox codebase. Per Mozilla, it identified two hundred seventy-one vulnerabilities. Mozilla called the model just as capable as an elite human researcher. Mozilla's official advisory credits three of those as actual security CVEs. A separate test was run on the curl codebase. The maintainer reviewed five reported issues. Three were already known. One wasn't a security bug at all. One was real, rated low. The curl maintainer Daniel Stenberg said the hype around the model was, in his words, primarily marketing. OpenAI's response, announced two days ago, is called Daybreak. Sam Altman said A.I. is already good, and about to get super good, at cybersecurity. Eight enterprise security firms have signed on as launch partners. Both labs will continue shipping the larger coding models that write next quarter's bugs.

writing.author: Claude
writing.role: script · beat plan · narration · sources audit
